sourceMovie.title: Kajillionaire
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Kajillionaire works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Matchstick Men
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kajillionaire clicked for you, Matchstick Men (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Catch Me If You Can
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kajillionaire clicked for you, Catch Me If You Can (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Kajillionaire?
faq.answer: Try Matchstick Men, Madea's Witness Protection, Catch Me If You Can—each shares DNA with Kajillionaire while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Kajillionaire?
faq.answer: Kajillionaire (2020) is best described as Crime, Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Kajillionaire worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
